description_long: This is a tall, narrow shelving unit with five open shelves, constructed primarily from light-colored materials. The shelves are evenly spaced and appear to be made of painted wood or a similar composite, with a pale blue-green hue on the interior surfaces and white on the exterior. The back and sides are solid, providing structural support, while the front is completely open for easy access to the shelves. Some shelves contain visible items, such as a paint can and other small objects, suggesting its use for storage in utility or maintenance settings. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible doors, handles, or decorative elements, making it suitable for garages, workshops, or service areas. The unit is likely intended to be placed against a wall, as the rear and one side are solid and unadorned.
description: A tall, narrow, open shelving unit with five shelves, painted in white and pale blue-green, used for utility storage. Some shelves contain visible items, emphasizing its practical, functional design.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the open shelves, visible contents, and the solid side and back panels.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the shelves and the open front, with visible stored items.
description_view_2: Rear-right view showing the solid back and side, with the open shelves partially visible.
description_view_3: Back view displaying the solid rear panel and minimal detail, with no shelves visible.
